vehicles.exclusionReason: Kia remains excluded as a live candidate; retained only as the performance, dashboard and ownership benchmark.
vehicles.availabilityUk: Benchmark only; current-model pricing and lease visibility deliberately not used for candidacy.

facts.audio: Meridian system in the prior benchmark vehicle.
facts.dashboard: Integrated curved dual-screen layout remains the positive ergonomics benchmark.
facts.phoneIntegration: Known personal benchmark; current-model reliability not refreshed.
facts.security: Theft experience makes it a negative security benchmark; current mitigations not scored.

notes: Reference vehicle only; not eligible for shortlist promotion.
Use its integrated display, acceleration and prior ownership experience as comparison points.
sources: Personal benchmark and established EV6 specification references.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in the UK; affected 2024-2025 cars remain subject to the UK high-voltage battery recall until inspected/repaired.

facts.audio: Harman Kardon soundbar system is available on higher trims.
facts.dashboard: Single 12.3-inch central display with very limited physical controls; major fit risk.
facts.phoneIntegration: Google built-in and wireless Apple CarPlay; OTA updates continue, but real-world software/display reliability remains a concern.
facts.security: Five-star crash safety, but theft/insurance/keyless mitigation evidence remains incomplete.

flags.featureSubscription: Google digital services are included for a limited period, then renewal terms may apply
flags.qualityConcern: UK battery-overheat recall affects 10,577 vehicles; affected cars must remain at or below 70% until repaired

notes: Remains research-only. Performance and audio are attractive, but the control layout is already a poor fit.
The official UK recall records a battery-cell overheating/fire risk and a temporary 70% charge limit until battery modules are inspected and replaced.
Do not arrange a test drive unless a dealer confirms the specific VIN is unaffected or fully repaired and demonstrates current software stability.
sources: Volvo UK recall information for EX30 R10360.
GOV.UK recall R/2025/559, published 22 January 2026.
Volvo UK EX30 software release notes.
Euro NCAP EX30 2024 result.

vehicles.origin: US brand, Europe-built, VW MEB platform
vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in the UK with stock, test-drive and leasing visibility. Ford currently advertises a £1,500 Electric Car Grant on eligible Explorer versions; exact Extended Range eligibility must be checked.

facts.audio: Optional 10-speaker B&O system with subwoofer and integrated soundbar.
facts.dashboard: 14.6-inch tilting portrait screen plus a separate 5.3-inch driver display; still screen-led but less crude than a freestanding tablet.
facts.phoneIntegration: Wireless smartphone integration is offered; reconnect reliability still lacks strong owner evidence.
facts.security: Five-star crash rating; theft resistance, keyless controls and tracker requirements remain unverified.

flags.grantEligibility: £1,500 grant is advertised, but confirm it applies to the desired Extended Range trim

notes: Value strengthens slightly because Ford is advertising grant support and active retail promotions, but no status movement is justified without a comparable salary-sacrifice quote.
Its weak point remains emotional appeal and performance relative to Smart/Audi/Cupra; the screen/control design still needs an in-person check.
Ready for a dealer or salary-sacrifice enquiry, specifically for an Extended Range car with B&O.
sources: Ford UK Explorer model page and promotions, checked 2026-07-17.
Ford UK price list effective 1 April 2026.
Euro NCAP Ford Explorer 2024 result.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in the UK from £51,800 with ordering, stock and test-drive visibility.

facts.audio: 20-speaker Sennheiser system with Dolby Atmos support.
facts.dashboard: Multi-screen hyperscreen-style fascia; visually integrated but highly software-dependent.
facts.phoneIntegration: CarPlay/Android Auto support is offered, but UK owner-level reconnect evidence remains thin.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP; theft, app security, insurance and tracker evidence remain incomplete.

flags.featureSubscription: Premium Connectivity Package is listed at £84 per 12 months and covers OTA maps, in-car apps and online services; third-party streaming subscriptions may also be required
flags.weakUkSupport: dealer network exists but model/software maturity is still young

notes: Remains the strongest test-drive candidate. Official UK specifications confirm 335 miles WLTP, 646 hp, 3.8 seconds to 62 mph and sub-18-minute charging under optimal conditions.
Subscription risk is now concrete rather than hypothetical: Smart lists an £84 annual Premium Connectivity Package for the #5.
The decisive test-drive risks remain software ergonomics, phone reconnection, ADAS intrusiveness, insurance/security and low-speed ride on UK roads.
sources: Smart UK #5 and BRABUS specifications, checked 2026-07-17.
Smart UK digital-products store for Premium Connectivity Package pricing.
Euro NCAP Smart #5 2025 result.
Recent European road tests on comfort, materials and charging.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in the UK; starting price remains well above the £55k purchase ceiling, though salary-sacrifice quotes may differ.

facts.audio: Bang & Olufsen 3D audio with head-rest speakers is pack dependent.
facts.dashboard: Integrated curved OLED panoramic display; premium but screen-heavy. Avoid virtual mirrors unless personally acceptable.
facts.phoneIntegration: Android Automotive platform with Apple CarPlay/Android Auto; reliability still needs owner evidence.
facts.security: Five-star crash rating; theft, keyless and insurer/tracker requirements still need direct confirmation.

flags.featureSubscription: verify Audi connected-feature and navigation renewal terms

notes: No status movement. It remains technically excellent but fails the purchase-price ceiling before options.
It can return to the shortlist only if a real salary-sacrifice quote makes the total cost competitive.
Dealer enquiry should focus on a non-virtual-mirror car with B&O and smaller wheels/comfort-oriented suspension.
sources: Audi UK A6 e-tron model information.
Euro NCAP A6 e-tron 2025 result.
Current UK pricing and road-test evidence rechecked 2026-07-17.

vehicles.availabilityUk: UK-configurable, but pricing starts well above the £55k ceiling and lease visibility remains limited compared with established models.

facts.audio: Bose on Plus and 25-speaker Bowers & Wilkins with Dolby Atmos on Ultra.
facts.dashboard: Large central touchscreen with a small driver display; more premium than EX30 but still strongly screen-led.
facts.phoneIntegration: Google built-in and wireless Apple CarPlay; long-term software reliability is not yet established.
facts.security: Extensive sensor suite, but no completed Euro NCAP result found and theft/insurance evidence is immature.
facts.euroNcap: Not yet confirmed in current evidence.

flags.featureSubscription: Google services renewal terms may apply after the included period

notes: Remains research-only: the sound, comfort, performance and charging case is excellent, but it fails the price ceiling and still lacks a confirmed Euro NCAP result.
Do not spend dealer time until a credible salary-sacrifice quote and five-star Euro NCAP result are available.
sources: Volvo UK ES90 model information.
Recent road tests praising the 25-speaker Bowers & Wilkins system and comfort.
Euro NCAP database rechecked 2026-07-17; no confirmed result retained.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in the UK with established Cupra dealer and lease visibility. Current official pricing is £54,930 for VZ1 and £59,830 for VZ2.

facts.audio: The 12-speaker Sennheiser system is standard on VZ2; official VZ2 pricing is £59,830, above budget.
facts.dashboard: Driver-oriented cockpit with a prominent central display and divisive centre spine.
facts.phoneIntegration: Apple CarPlay/Android Auto available; reconnect reliability still needs owner validation.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP; Kessy keyless entry and a mobile digital key are offered, but theft/insurance evidence remains unverified.

flags.featureSubscription: verify Cupra Connect renewal terms and feature policy
flags.theftSecurityConcern: Kessy and beta mobile digital key require direct insurance/security validation
flags.expensiveAudioTrim: VZ2 with Sennheiser is £59,830, above the £55k ceiling

notes: No formal status movement, but the value case weakens materially because the previous dashboard price understated the VZ range.
VZ1 is just inside the £55k ceiling at £54,930, while the Sennheiser-equipped VZ2 is £59,830. This creates a direct conflict between budget and the high-priority audio requirement.
A test drive remains justified only if a VZ1 can be demonstrated with acceptable audio or a salary-sacrifice quote makes VZ2 competitive.
sources: Cupra UK Tavascan 2026 configurator and trim pricing, checked 2026-07-17.
Euro NCAP Tavascan 2024 result.
Current UK lease visibility.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale with broad UK dealer and salary-sacrifice visibility; exact VZ transaction price varies by offer.

facts.audio: Premium audio is not a proven strength; verify current trim equipment.
facts.dashboard: Compact driver display plus central screen; current steering-wheel controls are improved over early cars but remain worth testing.
facts.phoneIntegration: CarPlay/Android Auto available; current software/reconnect reliability needs owner evidence.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP rating from the existing model generation; keyless/theft evidence still incomplete.

notes: No status movement. It remains the sensible value candidate, especially if salary-sacrifice pricing is strong.
It is ready for a dealer quote, but not an emotional front-runner because audio and performance are merely adequate relative to Smart/Audi.
sources: Cupra Born VZ specifications and current UK market listings.
Euro NCAP Cupra Born result.
Current UK lease visibility rechecked 2026-07-17.

vehicles.availabilityUk: Launched in the UK through MG dealers; entry pricing around £39,450 with growing lease visibility.

facts.dashboard: 26.3-inch panoramic display plus central screen; very high ergonomics risk for this shortlist.
facts.phoneIntegration: CarPlay/Android Auto support is reported, but UK reconnect and app reliability evidence remains weak.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP is now confirmed; theft, insurance, keyless and app-security evidence remains insufficient.

flags.touchOnlyBasicControls: high risk; reviews report mirror and other basic adjustments buried in menus
flags.theftSecurityConcern: high uncertainty despite five-star crash rating
flags.weakUkSupport: MG dealer network exists, but IM software/support maturity is unproven
flags.adasIntrusiveness: independent review evidence reports excessive and confusing safety alerts

notes: The missing-Euro-NCAP red flag was incorrect and has been removed: IM5 has a 2025 five-star rating with strong safety-assist performance.

That is a meaningful improvement, but not enough for promotion under the Chinese-brand evidence rule. Reviews still identify intrusive alerts, menu-buried controls and weaker perceived cabin quality than European rivals.
Keep in research; a dealer demo is useful only to validate software, controls, audio, ride and insurance/security.
sources: Euro NCAP IM5 2025 result: 89% adult, 85% child, 85% vulnerable-road-user and 87% safety-assist scores.
UK launch and pricing coverage for MG IM5.
Recent independent review evidence on intrusive ADAS and menu-heavy ergonomics.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale through MG dealers with UK pricing around £47,995 and visible leasing activity.

facts.dashboard: 26.3-inch panoramic display plus central screen; very high ergonomics risk.
facts.phoneIntegration: Support is reported, but real-world UK reconnect/app reliability evidence remains weak.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP is a positive; theft, insurance, keyless and app-security evidence remains incomplete.

flags.theftSecurityConcern: high uncertainty despite five-star crash rating
flags.weakUkSupport: MG dealer network exists, but IM software maturity remains unproven

notes: No status movement. UK price, availability and five-star Euro NCAP remain clear positives.
It remains in research because software, phone integration, security and control ergonomics are not strong enough to satisfy the evidence rule for a Chinese-brand candidate.
A dealer demo is useful only as research; do not treat it as purchase-ready yet.
